person_name: Alaric I, King of the Visigoths (r. 395-410)
person_name_full: Alaric I, King of the Visigoths (r. 395-410)
notes: King of the Visigoths who sacked Rome in 410 AD, noted for his leadership and interactions with Roman authorities.

docs.title: Letter 12020
docs.author: Cassiodorus
docs.subject_summary: Book 12, Letter XX: THOMATI ET PETRO VV. CC. ARCARIIS SENATOR PPO
docs.excerpt: But why should it be surprising that a man who enriched himself with such vast destruction of the city refused to plunder the venerable objects of the saints?

docs.title: New History, Book 5
docs.author: Zosimus
docs.excerpt: … he discovered that Alaric became seditious and disobedient to the laws, for he was displeased that he was not entrusted with the command of some other military forces besides the Barbarians, which Theodosius had allotted to him when he assisted in the deposition of the usurper Eugenius.

docs.title: New History, Book 6
docs.author: Zosimus
docs.excerpt: While Alaric continued faithful to the oath which he had given to Attalus, Valens, the commander of the cavalry, was arrested on suspicion of treason. Alaric in the mean time proceeded with his army to all the cities of Aemilia, which had refused to accept Attalus as their
